docs.title: City of Exiles
docs.text:
Welcome to the world of Erratt — a land still bleeding from the Schism, the day the Feywild tried to fold itself into the material plane. Magic and Fey spilled across the world, the ground itself was split, and the beasts of the forests woke into thought. Every border, grudge, and alliance that follows was drawn in that wound's shadow.

Our story begins at the edge of the map, in Utsa Paradisa — the Jewel of the Desert, the City of Exiles. It is a walled oasis where water is sacred because it is scarce, and where the cast-out of a hundred dead nations have come to build something like a home. This is the session archive and campaign wiki: a record of who the exiles are, what they've done, and the world that keeps testing them.

docs.title: The Schism
docs.text: The Schism was no war and no plague — it was the moment two worlds nearly became one. When the Feywild pressed against the material plane, it tore the land apart, flooded Erratt with raw magic and Fey denizens, and awakened ordinary forest animals into the sentient Beastfolk. The great powers that survived did so by adapting: dwarves and orcs ended centuries of mountain war with the Iron Grey Concordat, Drathomir carved new territory from the ashes of the Deadwood, and the Eladrin — blamed for it all — have been mistrusted ever since. Nothing in Erratt is untouched by it.

docs.title: The City of Exiles
docs.text:
Utsa Paradisa is home to those who could go nowhere else: predominantly Tieflings shunned by nations that no longer exist, a large Tabaxi population, and other exiled beastfolk. Here, control of water is control of everything — it is guarded, rationed, and revered, its rites tended at the Water Temple and its peace kept by the City Guard. From the crowded Bazaar and the tortoise-shell homes of the Slums to the wealth of the Jade District and the halls of the University, it is a city of outcasts carving out belonging amid scarcity and prejudice.

Utsa Paradisa punches far above its size. Empress Selene brokered the tenuous peace between Drathomir and the Goliaths, giving the city real diplomatic weight, and its lifeline runs north to UkRuundir, the Goliath capital — trading tar and spices for lumber and mammoth meat across a brutal desert-to-cold-forest crossing that few dare to make.

docs.title: The Wider World
docs.text: Beyond the desert lie the three great nations: cosmopolitan Trivan at the bottom of the Schism, the dwarven-and-orc Iron Holds, the dragonborn realm of Drathomir, and Medui-Ghislerin, the elven Tree of Five Families. Eastward stretch the Riverlands and the Emerald Wilderness, tended by halflings and firbolgs; northward, past Utsa Paradisa, sprawl the untamed Northern Territories. It is a crowded, quarrelsome world of old grudges and older gods — and the exiles are only just stepping into it.

docs.title: Sessions
docs.text: # Session Date 1 Session One — The Tidewater Amulet July 3, 2026

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: With Utsa Paradisa still reeling from a citywide assault, the party fought through the Slums and the Jade District to beat back an invasion of fey and elemental creatures. They saved a district and a family — but arrived at the Water Temple too late to stop the abduction of the water priestess Nybora Tidewater, who left behind a talking amulet that would set the party's next quest in motion.

docs.title: Key Events
docs.text:
• Aftermath and triage. In the ransacked Bazaar, the party freed Major Jamieson, a guard the suture flies had stitched together — hands sewn to his thighs, lips sewn shut — through a tense run of medicine checks and healing. The Major swore that his fellow guard Charles Gambit, who fled the fight, would be scrubbing his office when it was over.
  • Choosing the fight. The Griffin rider Cerus flew an aerial recon (natural 20) and laid out the city's wounds: the University held, the Slums were battered, and the Jade District — home of the Griffins and the Water Temple — was under the heaviest attack. Cerus peeled off to defend her Griffins; the party marched on the Slums, drawn by Behnsun Payne's orphanage and Adarius's parents.
  • The battle for the Slums. Amid the tortoise-shell homes, the party fought magma mephits, suture flies, and a thirster. A mephit's breath cone caught Adarius's father — critically — and burned half his face, but the hardy man survived. Patty Greenfoot and the Dragonborn Band — old rivals from a chariot race — turned up to buoy the party with inspiring music.
  • The Water Temple falls. Pressing on to the Jade District, the party found suture flies sewing Nybora Tidewater's mouth and hands shut. Despite the veteran commander Dalen Marr's stand, the flies lifted the priestess and carried her out of the city.
  • The dropped clue. As she was flown away, Nybora left behind a gold teardrop amulet. Adarius's identify revealed "something trapped inside" — a fey being that rejected him outright ("you were not chosen") and turned to Prance Galavant: "You will do. You must save my daughter." Prance swore an oath to the Amulet Speaker, put the amulet on, and felt "a spark of magic click."

docs.title: Memorable Moments
docs.text:
• Death from above. The party spent whole turns building a catapult mid-battle — a natural-20 contraption — and used it twice: first flinging Prance across the field, then launching Krum some 80 feet through the air to crash down on a fire mephit, driving both daggers through it and squashing the creature into the cracked earth.
  • A 39-damage flourish. Prance's brutal-critical longsword swing, delivered mid-monologue — "only when it's most dramatic."
  • "Healing is my game." Behnsun Payne introduced himself to the freed Major Jamieson in rhyme, then later froze a fire mephit with a chromatic orb of cold.
  • Talking to furniture. Fizz held his amulet Frederick up to the new one and "made them kiss," drawing drops of water from the teardrop — and learned that he, too, had been "chosen."
  • The world's grumpiest quest-giver. The amulet snapping at Adarius: "I have feelings, and my daughter was just kidnapped. I'm having a bad day."

docs.title: Open Threads
docs.text:
• Where is Nybora Tidewater? The suture flies carried her out of the city alive. Who sent them to silence a water-priestess — and to what end?
  • The amulet's nature. The Amulet Speaker claims to be Nybora's father, trapped in the teardrop. What is he, how was he bound, and what does it mean that both Prance and Fizz have been "chosen"?
  • Two chosen, two amulets. Frederick named the Tidewater spirit a "brother" but admitted he isn't sure he trusts him. How are the two fey vessels — and their bearers — connected?
  • The city's attackers. The fey and elemental creatures were beaten back "for now," but no one learned who unleashed them.
  • Adarius's dissent. Adarius rolled his way to genuine conviction that the amulet is bad news and still wants to sell it. That fault line isn't resolved.

docs.title: The Scene
docs.text:
The catapult had already thrown Prance once, and the party — half-mad on the sheer audacity of it — had built the thing back up for another shot. Krum climbed into the sling without a word of complaint, because a dwarf does not complain about being turned into ammunition; a dwarf simply checks that his daggers are loose in their sheaths. Below him the Slums burned in patches, the hollowed tortoise-shell homes throwing long shadows across the dust, and out among them a fire mephit hovered on crackling wings, chuckling that small fiendish chuckle that had been fraying everyone's nerves for an hour. "On three," Fizz called, bracing the frame. Krum did not wait for three.

He went up and the world went quiet. For one long breath there was only wind and the thump of his own heart and the whole ruined district tilting beneath him — eighty feet of desert air with a burning imp at the bottom of it. The mephit looked up a half-second too late. It had time to widen its ember eyes and no time at all to move.

Krum came down like a dropped anvil with a grudge. Both daggers led the way, punched to the hilt through the creature's molten hide, and his full dwarven weight followed after — driving the thing straight down, flattening it into the cracked earth in a spray of sparks and a smell of burnt tar. The fire went out of it all at once, snuffed under a hundred and some pounds of rogue. Krum wrenched his blades free, stood up in the little crater he'd made, and dusted off his shoulders while the embers settled around his boots. Somewhere behind him, Prance was already announcing that this, too, would make a magnificent verse.

docs.title: City Guard of Utsa Paradisa
docs.text: The guardsmen who keep order in Utsa Paradisa — stretched perilously thin during the citywide assault, and holding the city's districts by their fingernails.

docs.title: Notable Members
docs.text:
• Major Jamieson — an officer rallying the guard at the Bazaar after the party freed him from the suture flies.
  • Dalen Marr — veteran commander at the Water Temple, and Krum's direct superior.
  • Charles Gambit — a guardsman turned deserter, now in disgrace.

docs.title: Session History
docs.text: • The Tidewater Amulet — The guard fought and died in numbers across the Slums and the Water Temple, several withering to the thirsters' drought. The survivors, aided by the party, held the city — though they could not stop the priestess's abduction.

docs.title: The Bazaar
docs.text: The marketplace of Utsa Paradisa, where a bazaar ledger-man keeps the accounts. In an earlier session the party looted the Bazaar amid the chaos — and Behnsun Payne so frightened the ledger-man that he ate the ledger recording the stolen goods.

docs.title: Session History
docs.text: • The Tidewater Amulet — Left ransacked but no longer under immediate threat, held by Major Jamieson and the remaining guards while the party moved on to other districts.

docs.title: The Jade District
docs.text: Also called the J District — a district of Utsa Paradisa and the home base of the city's Griffins. The party started their campaign here and cleared part of it early on, but the stronger enemy force has since concentrated against it.

docs.title: Notable Features
docs.text:
• The roost of the city's Griffins, defended by riders such as Cerus.
  • The Water Temple, seat of the water priestess Nybora Tidewater.

docs.title: Session History
docs.text: • The Tidewater Amulet — Reported by Cerus's aerial scouting as the most resilient district but under the heaviest attack. Cerus flew to defend it while the party took the Slums first, then converged on the Water Temple here for the session's final battle.

docs.title: The Slums
docs.text: A poor district of Utsa Paradisa built inside the hollowed-out shells of ancient desert tortoises — "leviathan shells" from the giant beasts that once roamed this land, now repurposed as homes with shell "roofs" and elevated bridges between them.

docs.title: Notable Features
docs.text:
• An orphanage watched over by Behnsun Payne, home to the children Cadet Fox and Cadet Tiger.
  • A tavern run by the survivors of Behnsun's old crew.
  • The home of Adarius's parents.

docs.title: Who You'll Find Here
docs.text: Patty Greenfoot the gnome, local guardsmen, and the common folk who make up Fizz's fanbase.

docs.title: Session History
docs.text: • The Tidewater Amulet — The party chose to defend the Slums first, battling fire mephits, suture flies, and a thirster amid the tortoise shells. Adarius's father was gravely burned but survived; the Dragonborn Band arrived to turn the tide with music and healing. The district was left safe under Patty Greenfoot and the guard.

docs.title: The University
docs.text: A district of Utsa Paradisa home to the city's wizards and their tower. During the citywide assault it was the district that fared best, held by "a lot of strong wizards."

docs.title: Who You'll Find Here
docs.text: Krum's uncle lives here, and Adarius is said to have connections among its scholars.

docs.title: Session History
docs.text: • The Tidewater Amulet — Reported intact by Cerus's aerial scouting; shouts of victory rose from the Wizard Tower as the city was secured for the night.

docs.title: The Water Temple
docs.text: The temple in the Jade District of Utsa Paradisa where the water priestess Nybora Tidewater performs the rites that cleanse the city's water and keep it flowing. In a city where water is sacred and scarce, it is among the most vital sites in Utsa Paradisa.

docs.title: Session History
docs.text: • The Tidewater Amulet — The session's climactic battle. The party arrived — famously, by catapulting one another across the battlefield — to find suture flies stitching Nybora's mouth and hands shut. The guard commander Dalen Marr held the line, but the flies carried the priestess out of the city. Before she vanished she dropped a teardrop amulet to the party — the session's central clue.

docs.title: Utsa Paradisa — the City of Exiles
docs.text: A desert-oasis city in the Northern Territories of Erratt, and the campaign's home base. See world.md for the full setting.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Utsa Paradisa is a haven for the exiled — Tieflings, Tabaxi, and other beastfolk cast out by long-dead nations — ruled by Empress Selene, whose diplomacy brokered the fragile peace between Drathomir and the Goliaths. Here water is sacred and scarce, heavily guarded, and whoever controls it holds power.

docs.title: Districts
docs.text:
• The Bazaar — ransacked marketplace, held by the guard.
  • The Slums — tortoise-shell dwellings; home to an orphanage, a tavern, and Adarius's parents.
  • The Jade District — home of the Griffins and the Water Temple; under the heaviest attack.
  • The University — defended by strong wizards.
  • The Luxury District — the upscale quarter, where Prance Galavant has made connections.

docs.title: Current State
docs.text: As of The Tidewater Amulet, the city is in chaos — overrun by fey and elemental attackers (suture flies, thirsters, and fire mephits) in the aftermath of the previous session. The party fought district by district to beat back the assault, but the water priestess Nybora Tidewater was abducted from the Water Temple. The city is "safe for now."

docs.title: Cerus
docs.text: Role: Tiefling Griffin rider · Affiliation: the Griffin riders of the Jade District

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: A tiefling Griffin rider, addressed with courtly deference as "M'lady." She survived a brush with death in an earlier session — saved by Krum, while Prance so impressed her Griffin that the beast itself took a liking to the party.

docs.title: Session History
docs.text: • The Tidewater Amulet — At Prance's request, she took to the air and scouted the city (rolling a natural 20 on perception), reporting that the University stood strong, the Slums were worse off than usual, and the Jade District — home of the Griffins and the Water Temple — was under the heaviest attack. She then flew to defend the Jade District, sending the party toward the Slums.

docs.title: Charles Gambit
docs.text: Role: Guardsman of the City Guard of Utsa Paradisa

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: A city guard the party named in an earlier session, since revealed to be a coward. When the city came under attack he fled and hid rather than hold his post. Major Jamieson intends to make him an "office boy" as punishment, and Krum is no friend of his.

docs.title: Session History
docs.text: • The Tidewater Amulet — Mentioned only; still missing, having abandoned the Bazaar during the chaos.

docs.title: Dalen Marr
docs.text: Role: Veteran commander of the City Guard of Utsa Paradisa

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: A rugged, older guard commander — one of the elder officers, and the man Krum reports to directly. He fights with grim humor, standing his ground at the Water Temple and roaring at the attackers even when blinded by smoke: "I still see you, you pieces of shit."

docs.title: Session History
docs.text: • The Tidewater Amulet — Held the line at the Water Temple through the final battle, taking hits meant for the party and surviving the assault that carried off Nybora Tidewater.

docs.title: Frederick
docs.text: Role: Talking fey amulet carried by Fizz

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Frederick is a talking wooden amulet — a fey being bound into an idol — that Fizz carries and converses with. Only Fizz can hear him, which makes for some one-sided conversations with a piece of wood. Frederick is crotchety and comic, quick to withhold his trust, and speaks fondly (and protectively) of someone he calls "mom."

docs.title: Relationships
docs.text: • Recognized the spirit in Nybora Tidewater's teardrop — the Amulet Speaker — as a "brother," and confirmed that Fizz, like Prance, has been "chosen" by a fey vessel.

docs.title: Session History
docs.text: • The Tidewater Amulet — Vouched (grudgingly) for the new amulet and its quest, tying Fizz into the same fey mystery now driving Prance.

docs.title: Major Jamieson
docs.text: Role: Officer of the City Guard of Utsa Paradisa

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: A city guardsman who fell victim to the suture flies during the assault on Utsa Paradisa: his hands were sewn to his thighs and his lips stitched shut. The party found him panicking and freed him with a series of medicine checks and a healing touch.

docs.title: Personality
docs.text: Proud and duty-bound. The moment his mouth was freed — spitting blood — his first concern was rallying the remaining guards to hold the Bazaar. He has no patience for cowards: he wants the deserter Charles Gambit put to work as his "office boy" once the crisis passes.

docs.title: Session History
docs.text: • The Tidewater Amulet — Freed by Fizz, Behnsun Payne, and Prance Galavant, who unstitched his mouth and hand and healed his wounds while Krum kept him calm.

docs.title: Nybora Tidewater
docs.text: Role: Water Genasi priestess of Utsa Paradisa · Status: Abducted

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: A water Genasi priestess and one of the most important figures in the City of Exiles — by the DM's own reckoning, "besides the Empress, she may be the most important person in the city." Nybora cleanses the water when it is corrupted and prays to the goddess of water to keep it flowing into Utsa Paradisa, a city where water is sacred and scarce.

docs.title: Appearance
docs.text: A fish-like water Genasi. When the party found her at the Water Temple, her fingers were already burning from the assault.

docs.title: Session History
docs.text: • The Tidewater Amulet — Ambushed at the Water Temple by suture flies, which sewed her mouth and then her hands shut to stop her praying, and carried her bodily out of the city. As she was flown away, she tore a gold teardrop amulet from her chest and dropped it — deliberately — onto Prance Galavant's ear. The amulet holds a trapped fey being who calls her "my daughter" and begs the party to save her.

docs.title: Open Threads
docs.text:
• Where are the suture flies taking her, and why silence a water-priestess specifically?
  • The Amulet Speaker is her father. What is he, and how did he come to be trapped in the teardrop?

docs.title: Patty Greenfoot
docs.text: Role: Gnome of the Slums

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: A gnome and old friend of the party from the Slums. Once left injured and in pain, Patty had earlier helped Adarius and Krum reach the district. Warm and irrepressible, he grumbles about being too old to fight ("I should've blown in four years — what the hell am I doing out here?") even as he throws himself into the defense.

docs.title: Session History
docs.text: • The Tidewater Amulet — Fought alongside the party in the Slums and, when the battle turned, ran to shield the orphanage children. Afterward he pledged that he and the guards could hold the Slums, freeing the party to press on to the Water Temple.

docs.title: The Amulet Speaker
docs.text: Role: Fey being trapped in the Tidewater amulet · Status: Bound to Prance Galavant

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: A fey being imprisoned inside a gold, teardrop-shaped water amulet dropped by Nybora Tidewater as she was abducted. It speaks only to whoever holds it to their ear — where one hears the sound of ocean waves — and it is, by its own account, Nybora's father.

docs.title: Personality
docs.text: Prickly, grief-stricken, and imperious. It rejected Adarius flatly ("you were not chosen") and instead chose Prance ("You will do. You must save my daughter"). When Adarius called it names, it snapped back: "I have feelings, and my daughter was just kidnapped. I'm having a bad day."

docs.title: Relationships
docs.text:
• Father of Nybora Tidewater, the abducted water priestess.
  • Recognized by Frederick — Fizz's own fey amulet — as kin; the two spirits are "brothers," and Frederick confirmed that Fizz has also been "chosen."

docs.title: Session History
docs.text: • The Tidewater Amulet — Revealed by Adarius's identify as "something trapped inside." Chose Prance, who donned the amulet and swore an oath to rescue the priestess, feeling a spark of magic settle into place.

docs.title: The Dragonborn Band
docs.text: Role: Troupe of dragonborn musicians · Affiliation: wandering performers of Utsa Paradisa

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: A troupe of dragonborn troubadours the party first crossed at a chariot race — a red dragonborn singer with an Earth-Wind-and-Fire soul to his voice, a big dragonborn drummer, and a blue dragonborn among them. Old rivals turned allies, they remember the party as "the fuckers that fixed dark hearts" and admit the chariot race "was well played."

docs.title: Abilities
docs.text: When they play, their music grants inspiration (a d6 to add to a roll) to nearby fighters — and, pooling their magic once a day, they can lay hands and heal the wounded. Members trade element-nicknames: Earth, a hulking brown-scaled healer; Fire; and Water, who took a shine to Prance Galavant.

docs.title: Session History
docs.text: • The Tidewater Amulet — Arrived mid-battle in the Slums, buoying the party with inspiring music and healing Krum, Prance, and Adarius's badly burned father after the fight.

docs.title: Adarius
docs.text:
Player: Josh · Ancestry: Human · Class: Wizard

The party's arcane mind — sharp, skeptical, and rarely without a dry remark.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Adarius is the group's resident spellcaster and its loudest voice of caution. When the party recovered a mysterious talking amulet, it was Adarius who ritually cast identify on it — and Adarius who immediately wanted to sell the "talking asshole necklace" rather than answer its call.

docs.title: Ties to the City
docs.text: His parents live in the Slums of Utsa Paradisa — a hardy father the party came to know as "Bob" and a nimble mother. During the defense of the Slums, his father was caught in a magma creature's fire and badly burned, but survived thanks to the party and the healing magic of the Dragonborn Band. Adarius is also said to have connections at the University.

docs.title: Session History
docs.text: • The Tidewater Amulet — Cast identify on the teardrop amulet dropped by Nybora Tidewater, learning that "something is trapped inside" — a fey being that rejected him outright ("you were not chosen"). Spent the session urging the party to sell the amulet rather than take up its quest, and rolled a dirty nat 20 on Insight to remain thoroughly unconvinced.

docs.title: Behnsun Payne
docs.text:
Player: Jonathan · Ancestry: Human · Class: Sorcerer

"It's Behnsun Payne — and healing is my game." A gruff sorcerer with a soft spot he'd rather not admit to.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Behnsun leans on chill touch, chromatic orb, and firebolt, and keeps a rapier for flourish. His spell list ran thin against the fire creatures plaguing the city — most of them shrugged off flame — which forced him to get creative with cold and necrotic damage.

docs.title: Ties to the City
docs.text: Behnsun's roots are in the Slums. He looks after an orphanage there — two children, Cadet Fox and Cadet Tiger, call him "dad" — and has history with an old crew who run a tavern in the district. Last session he frightened a bazaar ledger-man badly enough that the man ate the ledger recording the party's stolen goods.

docs.title: Session History
docs.text: • The Tidewater Amulet — Helped free the stitched-together guard Major Jamieson, fought through the Slums to protect his orphanage, and finished a fire mephit with a well-chosen chromatic orb of cold that froze the creature before it could explode.

docs.title: Fizz
docs.text:
Player: Kevin · Ancestry: Myconid · Class: Bard

A friendly mushroom-man performer who measures success by how many friends he can make.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Fizz supports the party with bardic inspiration, song of rest, and a light crossbow, and carries a respectable medicine kit. He has a fanbase among the common folk of the Slums — "a lot of my customers," as he put it when the party chose where to make their stand.

docs.title: Frederick
docs.text: Fizz carries Frederick, a talking wooden amulet that houses a fey being. Over the course of the session it was revealed that Frederick has "chosen" Fizz — marking him, like Prance, as bound to a fey vessel and its purposes.

docs.title: Session History
docs.text: • The Tidewater Amulet — Freed Major Jamieson's sewn-shut mouth with a delicate medicine check, felled a suture fly with a clean crossbow shot, and used Frederick to confirm that both he and Prance had been "chosen" by kindred fey spirits.

docs.title: Krum
docs.text:
Player: Matt · Ancestry: Dwarf · Class: Rogue

A hardy dwarf rogue — low on hit points, high on nerve.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Krum fights with paired daggers and a crossbow, leaning on sneak attack and a rogue's cunning action to dart around the battlefield. He spent the session convinced he'd die in every encounter ("I made my peace with this") and came out the other side as its biggest hero.

docs.title: Ties to the City
docs.text: Krum has an uncle at the University, and is known to the guard — he reports directly to the veteran commander Dalen Marr. He is not on friendly terms with the coward Charles Gambit.

docs.title: Session History
docs.text: • The Tidewater Amulet — Twice let the party build a makeshift catapult and launch him across the battlefield to crash down on fire mephits, driving them so far into the earth that their death-bursts barely singed him. Also calmed the panicking Major Jamieson long enough for the party to unstitch him.

docs.title: Prance Galavant
docs.text:
Player: Gary · Ancestry: Harengon · Class: Paladin

A theatrical rabbit-knight who narrates his own legend as he lives it — "the greatest of heroes is here to improve the situation greatly."

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Prance is a Harengon paladin who fights with a two-handed longsword and split mail, heals allies with lay on hands, and leans on his rabbit traits — lucky footwork on failed saves and a knack for landing brutal critical hits. He speaks in grand, ceremonial flourishes and treats every fight as the next act of an unfolding story. Where others loot, Prance postures.

docs.title: The Tidewater Amulet
docs.text: When Nybora Tidewater was carried off, she draped a teardrop amulet over Prance's ear as a deliberate clue. The fey being inside chose him — "You will do. You must save my daughter." Against Adarius's strong objections, Prance donned the amulet and swore an oath to rescue the priestess, feeling "a spark of magic click" as he put it on.

docs.title: Session History
docs.text: • The Tidewater Amulet — Requested the aerial recon that set the party's course, delivered a 39-damage brutal-critical smashing hit in the Slums, was catapulted (with the rest) into the temple fight, and ended the night bound by oath to the amulet's quest.
